fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Illinois Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer physical therapist license to illinois, physical therapist compact illinois, illinois physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Illinois IDFPR — Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)
board.mailing_address:
Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
320 West Washington Street, 3rd Floor
Springfield, Illinois 62786

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Illinois.

steps.title: Review Requirements & Create Account
steps.description: Before applying, review the specific licensure requirements for Physical Therapists on the IDFPR website. Then, register for a new account on the IDFPR Online Services Portal.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your IDFPR Online Services Portal account, complete the online application for Physical Therapist licensure, and pay the required application fee of $200.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program, verification of any out-of-state licenses (if applicable), and official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores to be sent directly to IDFPR from the respective issuing bodies.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to the IDFPR Online Services Portal to check the status of your application and ensure all required documents have been received and processed by the Department.

steps.title: Receive License
steps.description: Once your application is complete and approved by the IDFPR, your Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Illinois physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $200. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Illinois have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Illinois
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Illinois, you must submit your application and supporting documentation through the IDFPR Online Services Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the IDFPR Online Services Portal, click 'Register for a New Account', complete the registration form with your personal details, and create your login credentials. Once registered, you can log in to start your application.
